original_hypothesis.title: setTokenOwner allows assigning multiple issuers to the same token, enabling conflicting vesting operations
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: VestingDeployer.setTokenOwner maps _tokenOwner[_owner] = token, but only checks that _owner doesn't already have a token. It does not prevent the same token address from being assigned to multiple owners. Each token owner can independently deploy vestings, create vesting allocations, and (critically) call transferVesting to move any user's vesting to an arbitrary address. Multiple token owners for the same token multiply the attack surface for fund confiscation.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Per the trust model, 'Token issuers are strictly assigned by the SecondSwap admin' and the system implies one issuer per token. The missing reverse-mapping check allows the admin to accidentally or intentionally create multiple issuers with full vesting transfer authority over the same user allocations.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No reverse mapping check in setTokenOwner; mapping is address→address (owner→token) with no token→owner tracking

proof_of_signal.pattern: setTokenOwner only checks _tokenOwner[_owner] == address(0), not whether token is already assigned to another owner

preconditions: Admin calls setTokenOwner(tokenX, ownerA) and setTokenOwner(tokenX, ownerB)
Both ownerA and ownerB now have token issuer privileges for tokenX

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: No uniqueness constraint on token address in _tokenOwner mapping
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Multiple token owners can independently call transferVesting for any user
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Secondary issuer can confiscate all user vestings for the shared token

economic_questions: Can admin remove or change a token owner assignment?
What is the maximum damage from a second token issuer?
Is there any on-chain detection mechanism for duplicate assignments?
validation_requirements: Call setTokenOwner twice with same token, different owners — confirm both succeed
Verify second owner can call transferVesting and createVesting on vestings deployed by first owner
Confirm no reverse mapping or uniqueness check exists

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: All vesting allocations for the affected token

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Anytime after admin creates duplicate assignment
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Total loss of trust for affected token's vesting plans

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All beneficiaries of vesting plans for the affected token
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All vestings for the affected token across all plans

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: _tokenOwner mapping structure and lack of reverse index
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm dual-assignment is possible and both owners retain full authority

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
